Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to recall what a normative Hire to Retire process should look like From the description it should be a linear sequence post job ad screen candidates interview make decision onboard add to payroll close case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Looking at Model 1 first The code shows a strict partial order with edges Post Screen Decide Screen Interview Decide Onboard Payroll Close

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: the anomaly here is that Interview happens after Screen but there is no edge from Interview to Decide In a standard process you would screen candidates then interview them then make a decision based on interviews But here Decide comes directly after Screen without Interview being a prerequisite That means someone could make a hiring decision without ever conducting interviews

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Also I notice Payroll is mandatory no choice structure which is correct since you must add new hires to payroll before closing But the bigger issue is the missing Interview Decide dependency

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now Model 2 is more complex It has a loop for onboarding either Onboard or skip via silent transition XOR for payroll either Payroll or skip Edges Post Screen and Post Interview parallel paths Post Screen Decide loop onboarding xor payroll Close But also Post Interview Decide

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The model allows two parallel paths after Post one through Screen and one through Interview Then both converge at Decide

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That seems problematic because You are conducting interviews without first screening candidates The standard process screens first to filter out unqualified applicants before interviewing The loop onboarding means onboarding could be skipped entirely via silent transition which does not make sense you cannot close a hiring case without onboarding a new employee Similarly xor payroll allows skipping payroll addition which is essential for paying the employee

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The parallel paths Screen and Interview both starting from Post are particularly odd In reality screening should happen before interviewing Having them as parallel branches means you could interview candidates who have not been screened which is inefficient and illogical

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Comparing the two models Model 1 main flaw is missing Interview Decide dependency causing decisions without interviews Model 2 has multiple severe flaws parallel screening and interviewing wrong order optional onboarding optional payroll addition

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 at least has the correct sequence from Post to Close with all activities mandatory just with a logical gap Model 2 has fundamental structural issues where critical steps can be skipped and the process flow is illogical parallel paths that should not be parallel

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore Model 1 is closer to normative despite its flaw because Model 2 violates more fundamental principles optional core activities incorrect parallelism

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The anomalies in Model 1 are less severe it is missing one dependency but still requires all activities in roughly correct order Model 2 allows skipping essential steps and has parallel paths that contradict hiring logic
